concerns.text: FR-006 and US-1 Edge Cases require excluding subjects with < 100 frames remaining after scrubbing. T007 only mentions filtering time series based on FD and logging exclusions, but lacks the explicit task to implement the < 100 frame count check and the logic to exclude the subject entirely (no entropy computed) as required.

Spec Edge Cases require flagging a subject for manual review if >10% of parcels are invalid (NaN). T015 mentions handling NaNs 'per FR-001', but FR-001 only mandates computing MSE. There is no specific task assigned to implement the >10% invalid parcel threshold check and the corresponding flagging/logging logic.

FR-005 and US-3 require a sensitivity analysis sweeping `r` over {0.15, 0.20, 0.25}*SD. T030 describes re-running entropy and modeling, but does not explicitly mandate re-computing the Area Under the Curve (AUC) across multiple scales (FR-009) for each `r` value. Without this, the 'Multiscale' aspect of the metric is not validated across the sensitivity sweep.

SC-003 requires reporting peak RAM usage and runtime to verify compute feasibility. T030 (sensitivity sweep) lacks a specific step to instrument and log these performance metrics during the re-computation, risking failure to validate the 'Compute feasibility' assumption in the spec.

Spec Edge Cases require halting execution and logging a critical warning if N < 30. T025 mentions implementing sample size validation, but the task description is brief. It should explicitly ensure the logic halts the primary analysis (not just warns) to prevent generating misleading p-values, as per the spec's strict handling.
